problems.stem: She graduated / has graduated from university three years ago.

problems.stem: He worked / has worked as a teacher for 20 years before he retired.

                                                have heard

다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

This allows for deeper concentration and ④ ultimately produces higher quality work than task-switching ⑤ does.

다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

The concept of 'placebo effect' is fascinating. It occurs when a patient experiences real improvements in their condition after ① receiving a treatment with no active therapeutic substance, like a sugar pill. The true wonder of the wall ④ lies not in its visibility from space, but in the immense human effort it represents, a testament to the perseverance of ⑤ that who built it.

The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 is a classic example of serendipity in science. Fleming had left a petri dish containing bacteria ⓐ contaminating by a mold. He noticed that the bacteria around the mold ⓑ had been killed. This observation led him ⓒ to isolate the substance responsible: penicillin. However, it was Howard Florey and Ernst Chain who later developed a method to mass-produce the drug. Without their work, Fleming's discovery might have remained a scientific curiosity. This story shows that scientific breakthroughs often require not only a keen observation but also the dedication ⓓ to develop that observation into a practical application.

다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

Human beings differ in many ways and have a variety of tastes; different things appeal more to one person than to ① another. There is no particular book or group of books that must be great for you, but you should seek out the ⑤ few books that have value for you.

다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

One of the most significant advances during the Renaissance ⓐ were the development of a new attitude toward the acquisition of knowledge itself. When Copernicus, on the basis of his observations, suggested that the Sun, and not the Earth, ⓒ be the point about which the planets revolved, the Church banned his book. Galileo demonstrated that the planets are held in their orbits by physical laws, which led to ⓓ him spending the last eight years of his life under house arrest.

다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

The neurotransmitter dopamine moves between neurons in different areas of the brain that ⓐ control the way in which people sleep, eat, and move. Their brains crave more and more dopamine while producing less dopamine naturally.

The Dunning-Kruger effect is a cognitive bias where people with low ability at a task overestimate their ability. It is related to the metacognitive inability of the unskilled to recognize their own ineptitude. This is (A) because / why they cannot objectively evaluate their own performance. Conversely, highly skilled individuals may underestimate their relative competence, erroneously assuming that tasks which are easy for them (B) is / are also easy for others. 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

The imposter syndrome is a psychological pattern in which an individual doubts their accomplishments and has a persistent internalized fear of ① being exposed as a "fraud." Despite external evidence of their competence, those experiencing this syndrome remain convinced that they are frauds and do not deserve all they have achieved. They often attribute their success to luck or to ② deceiving others into thinking they are more intelligent than they perceive themselves to be. It is important that anyone suffering from this ③ seeks professional help, as it can lead to anxiety and depression. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

The concept of "chunking" in psychology refers to the process of taking individual pieces of information and grouping them into larger units. By grouping each piece into a large whole, you can improve the amount of information you can remember. For example, a phone number sequence of 4-7-1-1-3-2-4 is difficult to remember. However, if you chunk it into 471-1324, it becomes much easier. This is because our short-term memory is only capable of holding a limited number of items, typically around seven. Chunking allows us ⓐ overcoming this limitation by making each item ⓑ contain more information. This strategy ⓒ is used widely in everyday life, from memorizing credit card numbers ⓓ to learn new languages.

다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?

The bystander effect is a social psychological phenomenon where individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present. The greater the number of bystanders, the less likely it is that any one of them ① will help. Several factors contribute to this. One is diffusion of responsibility: when others are present, individuals feel less personal responsibility to act, ② assumed someone else will. Another is the need to behave in socially acceptable ways; if other people are not reacting, individuals often interpret the situation as a non-emergency. This makes ③ it crucial to single out an individual in a crowd when you need help. The "wisdom of the crowd" refers to the idea that large groups of people are collectively smarter than individual experts. 다음 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 틀린 것을 두 개 찾아 기호를 쓰고 바르게 고치시오.

The human memory is not like a video camera, ⓐ recording events exactly as they happened. Instead, it is a reconstructive process that is susceptible to distortion. The availability heuristic is a mental shortcut that relies on immediate examples that come to a person's mind when evaluating a specific topic. Because of this, we often misjudge the frequency of events. For example, after seeing several news reports about shark attacks, you might feel that the likelihood of ① being attacked by a shark is quite high. In reality, you are far more likely to be harmed by a falling coconut. To make more accurate judgments, it is important ④ to consider statistical data rather than ⑤ relying solely on memorable anecdotes.

The fight-or-flight response is a physiological reaction that occurs in response to a perceived harmful event.
